title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
excerpt: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.
content_html:
Etched, the AI chip startup founded by three Harvard dropouts in 2022, has closed a $300 million Series C funding round at a $10.3 billion valuation, co-founder and COO Robert Wachen tells TechCrunch.  

The round was led by Sequoia, with Andreessen Horowitz, SK Hynix, Jane Street, and Diffusion Capital also participating, along with other, earlier investors. Other backers of the company include names like Peter Thiel, Andrej Karpathy, Dylan Field, Amjad Masad, and more. 

Etched was previously valued at $5 billion in December when it raised a $500 million round, meaning it has doubled its valuation in about seven months. The company says this is the highest valuation ever for a Sequoia-led Series C. Last month, Etched announced that it had successfully manufactured its homegrown chips, that its first full systems were being tested by clients, and it had already booked $1 billion worth of orders. 

Etched launched at a time when the idea of building a chip specifically for AI models based on transformer technology (the architecture behind most modern AI systems, including ChatGPT and Claude) was considered wild if not wacky. The company is still battling the perception that its products — which are sold as full systems, not just chips — involve chips designed to run only specific LLMs.  

That’s not the case, Wachen explains. The systems can run any AI model, including Mixture of Experts models like DeepSeek and Qwen — an architecture that splits tasks across specialized sub-models rather than relying on one large model — as well as non-transformer designs like Mamba, which is built on a different underlying architecture known as a state-space model. (Interestingly, the idea of etching parts of a specific AI model directly into silicon to boost performance isn’t considered far-fetched anymore. Google is reportedly pursuing the same concept with its Frozen v2 chip for Gemini.)

Still, Etched’s claim to fame today is that it designed two new components from scratch to speed up inference — the computing process that happens after a user submits a prompt.  

“Inference is built in two stages,” Wachen says, “prefill and decode.” The “prefill phase” involves understanding the prompt, including context. It’s mathematically and compute-intensive. The “decode” phase generates the output tokens (the actual answer the user sees). It requires less computation but needs massive amounts of memory. 

Etched created a prefill chip that operates “dramatically” faster, he promises, “by running at a much lower voltage than any other AI chip. We call this low-voltage inference.” Lower voltage generates less heat, which allows the chip to pack in more transistors.  

For the decode process, Etched created a new type of memory and “interconnect technology that we call cluster-scale memory. It allows many chips to connect together and use a shared memory pool at a very, very fast, low latency,” he says. The result, Etched promises, is high speeds but lower costs.

Because the startup was launched before most of the tech world (besides Nvidia) understood AI’s specialized compute needs, the founders, CEO Gavin Uberti, Wachen, and Chris Zhu, have faced plenty of skeptics who kept doubting even after the company announced that its first batch of silicon had been successfully manufactured by TSMC.

Much of that comes from how few people have had access to the systems. So far, access has been limited to investors and early customers. In fact, that’s how Etched landed its list of famous investors in the first place — by showing them private demos in its office.

“Andrej Karpathy from Anthropic, Noam Brown from OpenAI, Geoffrey Hinton, as well as all the investors in the funding round — these are all people who actually tried the hardware and are very excited about it,” Wachen says.  

Still, it’s been a long, difficult road with more to go until the rack systems are mass produced and delivered. The trio famously dropped out of Harvard to launch Etched, not knowing then how to raise cash (much less the loads of it they would need) or how to hire. 

“We had no idea how hard it was going to be,” Wachen says. “I think we still have to be humbled by what it will take to actually get to scale.” 

Wachen remembers landing in the Bay Area after telling his parents he was leaving school to do a startup, with no office or apartment arranged. He slept on the floor of a friend’s unfurnished house. 

“I remember staying in my friend’s house that they were about to sell, using a towel as a blanket,” he laughs. The founders eventually set up the servers they needed to run the chip-design tools in the garage of an early employee and “every time it needed to be rebooted, he would call his wife, and she would go and hit the reboot button.” 

Today, there are 400 people bustling in an office, and Etched operates a 2 megawatt data center there. And it just opened a new 80,000 square-foot, 10MW facility in Milpitas, down the road from its main San Jose office.

“We’re running tokens in our lab today, working with some of the largest AI companies in the world,” he says. 

Wachen also has a blanket now, and a mattress “and a pillow even. Multiple pillows,” he jokes.  

More importantly, he and his co-founders never let the doubters stop them. “It’s come a long way. It’s a very, very different world. But I think, when you really think something’s possible, and you just work at it for a long time, you can do it.”

Note: This story was updated to include a reference to the new 10MW facility.

When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission. This doesn’t affect our editorial independence.

blocks.html_en: Etched, the AI chip startup founded by three Harvard dropouts in 2022, has closed a $300 million Series C funding round at a $10.3 billion valuation, co-founder and COO Robert Wachen tells TechCrunch.

sentences.en: Etched, the AI chip startup founded by three Harvard dropouts in 2022, has closed a $300 million Series C funding round at a $10.3 billion valuation, co-founder and COO Robert Wachen tells TechCrunch.
sentences.zh: 联合创始人兼首席运营官 Robert Wachen 告诉 TechCrunch，由三名哈佛辍学生于 2022 年创立的 AI 芯片初创公司 Etched 已完成 3 亿美元的 C 轮融资，估值为 103 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: The round was led by Sequoia, with Andreessen Horowitz, SK Hynix, Jane Street, and Diffusion Capital also participating, along with other, earlier investors. Other backers of the company include names like Peter Thiel, Andrej Karpathy, Dylan Field, Amjad Masad, and more.

sentences.en: The round was led by Sequoia, with Andreessen Horowitz, SK Hynix, Jane Street, and Diffusion Capital also participating, along with other, earlier investors.
sentences.zh: 此轮融资由红杉资本领投，Andreessen Horowitz、SK Hynix、Jane Street、Diffusion Capital 以及其他早期投资者也参与其中。

sentences.en: Other backers of the company include names like Peter Thiel, Andrej Karpathy, Dylan Field, Amjad Masad, and more.
sentences.zh: 该公司的其他支持者包括 Peter Thiel、Andrej Karpathy、Dylan Field、Amjad Masad 等。

blocks.html_en: Etched was previously valued at $5 billion in December when it raised a $500 million round, meaning it has doubled its valuation in about seven months. The company says this is the highest valuation ever for a Sequoia-led Series C. Last month, Etched announced that it had successfully manufactured its homegrown chips, that its first full systems were being tested by clients, and it had already booked $1 billion worth of orders.

sentences.en: Etched was previously valued at $5 billion in December when it raised a $500 million round, meaning it has doubled its valuation in about seven months.
sentences.zh: Etched 此前在去年 12 月筹集了 5 亿美元资金时估值为 50 亿美元，这意味着它的估值在大约七个月内翻了一番。

sentences.en: The company says this is the highest valuation ever for a Sequoia-led Series C.
sentences.zh: 该公司表示，这是红杉资本主导的 C 轮融资以来的最高估值。

sentences.en: Last month, Etched announced that it had successfully manufactured its homegrown chips, that its first full systems were being tested by clients, and it had already booked $1 billion worth of orders.
sentences.zh: 上个月，Etched 宣布它已经成功制造了自己的芯片，其首个完整系统正在接受客户测试，并且已经预订了价值 10 亿美元的订单。

blocks.html_en: Etched launched at a time when the idea of building a chip specifically for AI models based on transformer technology (the architecture behind most modern AI systems, including ChatGPT and Claude) was considered wild if not wacky. The company is still battling the perception that its products — which are sold as full systems, not just chips — involve chips designed to run only specific LLMs.

sentences.en: Etched launched at a time when the idea of building a chip specifically for AI models based on transformer technology (the architecture behind most modern AI systems, including ChatGPT and Claude) was considered wild if not wacky.
sentences.zh: Etched 推出之际，专门为基于 Transformer 技术（大多数现代人工智能系统背后的架构，包括 ChatGPT 和 Claude）的 AI 模型构建芯片的想法被认为是疯狂的，甚至是古怪的。

sentences.en: The company is still battling the perception that its products — which are sold as full systems, not just chips — involve chips designed to run only specific LLMs.
sentences.zh: 该公司仍在与这样的看法作斗争，即其产品（作为完整系统而不仅仅是芯片出售）涉及仅运行特定法学硕士的芯片。

blocks.html_en: That’s not the case, Wachen explains. The systems can run any AI model, including Mixture of Experts models like DeepSeek and Qwen — an architecture that splits tasks across specialized sub-models rather than relying on one large model — as well as non-transformer designs like Mamba, which is built on a different underlying architecture known as a state-space model. (Interestingly, the idea of etching parts of a specific AI model directly into silicon to boost performance isn’t considered far-fetched anymore. Google is reportedly pursuing the same concept with its Frozen v2 chip for Gemini.)

sentences.en: That’s not the case, Wachen explains.
sentences.zh: 瓦亨解释说，事实并非如此。

sentences.en: The systems can run any AI model, including Mixture of Experts models like DeepSeek and Qwen — an architecture that splits tasks across specialized sub-models rather than relying on one large model — as well as non-transformer designs like Mamba, which is built on a different underlying architecture known as a state-space model.
sentences.zh: 这些系统可以运行任何人工智能模型，包括像 DeepSeek 和 Qwen 这样的专家混合模型（一种将任务分配给专门的子模型而不是依赖于一个大型模型的架构）以及像 Mamba 这样的非变压器设计，它构建在称为状态空间模型的不同底层架构上。

sentences.en: (Interestingly, the idea of etching parts of a specific AI model directly into silicon to boost performance isn’t considered far-fetched anymore.
sentences.zh: （有趣的是，将特定人工智能模型的一部分直接蚀刻到硅中以提高性能的想法不再被认为是牵强的。

sentences.en: Google is reportedly pursuing the same concept with its Frozen v2 chip for Gemini.)
sentences.zh: 据报道，谷歌正在为 Gemini 的 Frozen v2 芯片追求同样的概念。）

blocks.html_en: Still, Etched’s claim to fame today is that it designed two new components from scratch to speed up inference — the computing process that happens after a user submits a prompt.

sentences.en: Still, Etched’s claim to fame today is that it designed two new components from scratch to speed up inference — the computing process that happens after a user submits a prompt.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，Etched 如今之所以出名，是因为它从头开始设计了两个新组件，以加快推理速度，即用户提交提示后发生的计算过程。

blocks.html_en: “Inference is built in two stages,” Wachen says, “prefill and decode.” The “prefill phase” involves understanding the prompt, including context. It’s mathematically and compute-intensive. The “decode” phase generates the output tokens (the actual answer the user sees). It requires less computation but needs massive amounts of memory.

sentences.en: “Inference is built in two stages,” Wachen says, “prefill and decode.”
sentences.zh: Wachen 说：“推理分两个阶段进行：预填充和解码。”

sentences.en: The “prefill phase” involves understanding the prompt, including context.
sentences.zh: “预填充阶段”涉及理解提示，包括上下文。

sentences.en: It’s mathematically and compute-intensive.
sentences.zh: 它是数学和计算密集型的。

sentences.en: The “decode” phase generates the output tokens (the actual answer the user sees).
sentences.zh: “解码”阶段生成输出令牌（用户看到的实际答案）。

sentences.en: It requires less computation but needs massive amounts of memory.
sentences.zh: 它需要较少的计算，但需要大量的内存。

blocks.html_en: Etched created a prefill chip that operates “dramatically” faster, he promises, “by running at a much lower voltage than any other AI chip. We call this low-voltage inference.” Lower voltage generates less heat, which allows the chip to pack in more transistors.

sentences.en: Etched created a prefill chip that operates “dramatically” faster, he promises, “by running at a much lower voltage than any other AI chip.
sentences.zh: Etched 创造了一种预填充芯片，其运行速度“显着”更快，他承诺，“通过比任何其他人工智能芯片低得多的电压运行。

sentences.en: We call this low-voltage inference.”
sentences.zh: 我们称之为低压推理。”

sentences.en: Lower voltage generates less heat, which allows the chip to pack in more transistors.
sentences.zh: 较低的电压产生的热量较少，这使得芯片可以容纳更多的晶体管。

blocks.html_en: For the decode process, Etched created a new type of memory and “interconnect technology that we call cluster-scale memory. It allows many chips to connect together and use a shared memory pool at a very, very fast, low latency,” he says. The result, Etched promises, is high speeds but lower costs.

sentences.en: For the decode process, Etched created a new type of memory and “interconnect technology that we call cluster-scale memory.
sentences.zh: 对于解码过程，Etched 创建了一种新型存储器和“互连技术”，我们称之为集群规模存储器。

sentences.en: It allows many chips to connect together and use a shared memory pool at a very, very fast, low latency,” he says.
sentences.zh: 它允许许多芯片连接在一起，并以非常非常快、低延迟的速度使用共享内存池，”他说。

sentences.en: The result, Etched promises, is high speeds but lower costs.
sentences.zh: Etched 承诺，其结果是速度高但成本更低。

sentences.en: Etched founders Rob Wachen (seated, middle) and Gavin Uberti (standing, right) with Sequoia partners Abishek Malani (far left) and Sonya Huang (middle).Image Credits:Etched
sentences.zh: Etched 创始人 Rob Wachen（坐，中）和 Gavin Uberti（站，右）与红杉合伙人 Abishek Malani（最左）和 Sonya Huang（中）。 图片来源：Etched

blocks.html_en: Because the startup was launched before most of the tech world (besides Nvidia) understood AI’s specialized compute needs, the founders, CEO Gavin Uberti, Wachen, and Chris Zhu, have faced plenty of skeptics who kept doubting even after the company announced that its first batch of silicon had been successfully manufactured by TSMC.

sentences.en: Because the startup was launched before most of the tech world (besides Nvidia) understood AI’s specialized compute needs, the founders, CEO Gavin Uberti, Wachen, and Chris Zhu, have faced plenty of skeptics who kept doubting even after the company announced that its first batch of silicon had been successfully manufactured by TSMC.
sentences.zh: 由于这家初创公司是在大多数科技界（Nvidia 除外）了解人工智能的专业计算需求之前成立的，因此创始人、首席执行官 Gavin Uberti、Wachen 和 Chris Zhu 面临着许多怀疑论者，即使在该公司宣布其第一批芯片已由台积电成功制造后，他们仍然持怀疑态度。

blocks.html_en: Much of that comes from how few people have had access to the systems. So far, access has been limited to investors and early customers. In fact, that’s how Etched landed its list of famous investors in the first place — by showing them private demos in its office.

sentences.en: Much of that comes from how few people have had access to the systems.
sentences.zh: 其中很大一部分原因是能够访问这些系统的人太少了。

sentences.en: So far, access has been limited to investors and early customers.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，访问权限仅限于投资者和早期客户。

sentences.en: In fact, that’s how Etched landed its list of famous investors in the first place — by showing them private demos in its office.
sentences.zh: 事实上，这就是 Etched 最初获得著名投资者名单的方式——在其办公室向他们展示私人演示。

blocks.html_en: “Andrej Karpathy from Anthropic, Noam Brown from OpenAI, Geoffrey Hinton, as well as all the investors in the funding round — these are all people who actually tried the hardware and are very excited about it,” Wachen says.

sentences.en: “Andrej Karpathy from Anthropic, Noam Brown from OpenAI, Geoffrey Hinton, as well as all the investors in the funding round — these are all people who actually tried the hardware and are very excited about it,” Wachen says.
sentences.zh: Wachen 表示：“Anthropic 的 Andrej Karpathy、OpenAI 的 Noam Brown、Geoffrey Hinton 以及本轮融资的所有投资者——这些人都实际尝试过硬件，并且对此感到非常兴奋。”

blocks.html_en: Still, it’s been a long, difficult road with more to go until the rack systems are mass produced and delivered. The trio famously dropped out of Harvard to launch Etched, not knowing then how to raise cash (much less the loads of it they would need) or how to hire.

sentences.en: Still, it’s been a long, difficult road with more to go until the rack systems are mass produced and delivered.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，在机架系统大规模生产和交付之前，这仍然是一条漫长而艰难的道路，还有更多的路要走。

sentences.en: The trio famously dropped out of Harvard to launch Etched, not knowing then how to raise cash (much less the loads of it they would need) or how to hire.
sentences.zh: 这三人从哈佛退学并创立了 Etched，当时他们不知道如何筹集现金（更不用说他们需要的大量资金）或如何招聘。

blocks.html_en: “We had no idea how hard it was going to be,” Wachen says. “I think we still have to be humbled by what it will take to actually get to scale.”

sentences.en: “We had no idea how hard it was going to be,” Wachen says.
sentences.zh: “我们不知道这会有多困难，”瓦亨说。

sentences.en: “I think we still have to be humbled by what it will take to actually get to scale.”
sentences.zh: “我认为我们仍然需要对真正实现规模化所需要的条件感到谦卑。”

blocks.html_en: Wachen remembers landing in the Bay Area after telling his parents he was leaving school to do a startup, with no office or apartment arranged. He slept on the floor of a friend’s unfurnished house.

sentences.en: Wachen remembers landing in the Bay Area after telling his parents he was leaving school to do a startup, with no office or apartment arranged.
sentences.zh: 瓦亨记得在告诉父母他要离开学校去创业后降落在湾区，没有安排办公室或公寓。

sentences.en: He slept on the floor of a friend’s unfurnished house.
sentences.zh: 他睡在一个朋友没有家具的房子的地板上。

blocks.html_en: “I remember staying in my friend’s house that they were about to sell, using a towel as a blanket,” he laughs. The founders eventually set up the servers they needed to run the chip-design tools in the garage of an early employee and “every time it needed to be rebooted, he would call his wife, and she would go and hit the reboot button.”

sentences.en: “I remember staying in my friend’s house that they were about to sell, using a towel as a blanket,” he laughs.
sentences.zh: “我记得住在我朋友即将出售的房子里，用毛巾当毯子，”他笑着说。

sentences.en: The founders eventually set up the servers they needed to run the chip-design tools in the garage of an early employee and “every time it needed to be rebooted, he would call his wife, and she would go and hit the reboot button.”
sentences.zh: 创始人最终在一名早期员工的车库中设置了运行芯片设计工具所需的服务器，“每次需要重新启动时，他都会打电话给他的妻子，她会去按下重新启动按钮。”

blocks.html_en: Today, there are 400 people bustling in an office, and Etched operates a 2 megawatt data center there. And it just opened a new 80,000 square-foot, 10MW facility in Milpitas, down the road from its main San Jose office.

sentences.en: Today, there are 400 people bustling in an office, and Etched operates a 2 megawatt data center there.
sentences.zh: 如今，办公室里有 400 名员工忙碌，Etched 在那里运营着一个 2 兆瓦的数据中心。

sentences.en: And it just opened a new 80,000 square-foot, 10MW facility in Milpitas, down the road from its main San Jose office.
sentences.zh: 该公司刚刚在米尔皮塔斯开设了一座占地 80,000 平方英尺、装机容量为 10 兆瓦的新工厂，位于圣何塞主要办事处的附近。

blocks.html_en: “We’re running tokens in our lab today, working with some of the largest AI companies in the world,” he says.

sentences.en: “We’re running tokens in our lab today, working with some of the largest AI companies in the world,” he says.
sentences.zh: “我们今天在实验室里运行代币，与世界上一些最大的人工智能公司合作，”他说。

blocks.html_en: Wachen also has a blanket now, and a mattress “and a pillow even. Multiple pillows,” he jokes.

sentences.en: Wachen also has a blanket now, and a mattress “and a pillow even.
sentences.zh: Wachen 现在也有了一条毯子、一张床垫，甚至还有一个枕头。

sentences.en: Multiple pillows,” he jokes.
sentences.zh: 多个枕头，”他开玩笑说。

blocks.html_en: More importantly, he and his co-founders never let the doubters stop them. “It’s come a long way. It’s a very, very different world. But I think, when you really think something’s possible, and you just work at it for a long time, you can do it.”

sentences.en: More importantly, he and his co-founders never let the doubters stop them.
sentences.zh: 更重要的是，他和他的联合创始人从未让怀疑者阻止他们。

sentences.en: “It’s come a long way.
sentences.zh: “已经走了很长一段路了。

sentences.en: It’s a very, very different world.
sentences.zh: 这是一个非常非常不同的世界。

sentences.en: But I think, when you really think something’s possible, and you just work at it for a long time, you can do it.”
sentences.zh: 但我认为，当你真的认为某件事是可能的，并且你只要长期努力，你就能做到。”

sentences.en: Note: This story was updated to include a reference to the new 10MW facility.
sentences.zh: 注：本文已更新，包含对新 10MW 设施的引用。

content_html_zh:
联合创始人兼首席运营官 Robert Wachen 告诉 TechCrunch，由三名哈佛辍学生于 2022 年创立的 AI 芯片初创公司 Etched 已完成 3 亿美元的 C 轮融资，估值为 103 亿美元。

此轮融资由红杉资本领投，Andreessen Horowitz、SK Hynix、Jane Street、Diffusion Capital 以及其他早期投资者也参与其中。 该公司的其他支持者包括 Peter Thiel、Andrej Karpathy、Dylan Field、Amjad Masad 等。

Etched 此前在去年 12 月筹集了 5 亿美元资金时估值为 50 亿美元，这意味着它的估值在大约七个月内翻了一番。 该公司表示，这是红杉资本主导的 C 轮融资以来的最高估值。 上个月，Etched 宣布它已经成功制造了自己的芯片，其首个完整系统正在接受客户测试，并且已经预订了价值 10 亿美元的订单。

Etched 推出之际，专门为基于 Transformer 技术（大多数现代人工智能系统背后的架构，包括 ChatGPT 和 Claude）的 AI 模型构建芯片的想法被认为是疯狂的，甚至是古怪的。 该公司仍在与这样的看法作斗争，即其产品（作为完整系统而不仅仅是芯片出售）涉及仅运行特定法学硕士的芯片。

瓦亨解释说，事实并非如此。 这些系统可以运行任何人工智能模型，包括像 DeepSeek 和 Qwen 这样的专家混合模型（一种将任务分配给专门的子模型而不是依赖于一个大型模型的架构）以及像 Mamba 这样的非变压器设计，它构建在称为状态空间模型的不同底层架构上。 （有趣的是，将特定人工智能模型的一部分直接蚀刻到硅中以提高性能的想法不再被认为是牵强的。 据报道，谷歌正在为 Gemini 的 Frozen v2 芯片追求同样的概念。）

尽管如此，Etched 如今之所以出名，是因为它从头开始设计了两个新组件，以加快推理速度，即用户提交提示后发生的计算过程。

Wachen 说：“推理分两个阶段进行：预填充和解码。” “预填充阶段”涉及理解提示，包括上下文。 它是数学和计算密集型的。 “解码”阶段生成输出令牌（用户看到的实际答案）。 它需要较少的计算，但需要大量的内存。

Etched 创造了一种预填充芯片，其运行速度“显着”更快，他承诺，“通过比任何其他人工智能芯片低得多的电压运行。 我们称之为低压推理。” 较低的电压产生的热量较少，这使得芯片可以容纳更多的晶体管。

对于解码过程，Etched 创建了一种新型存储器和“互连技术”，我们称之为集群规模存储器。 它允许许多芯片连接在一起，并以非常非常快、低延迟的速度使用共享内存池，”他说。 Etched 承诺，其结果是速度高但成本更低。

由于这家初创公司是在大多数科技界（Nvidia 除外）了解人工智能的专业计算需求之前成立的，因此创始人、首席执行官 Gavin Uberti、Wachen 和 Chris Zhu 面临着许多怀疑论者，即使在该公司宣布其第一批芯片已由台积电成功制造后，他们仍然持怀疑态度。

其中很大一部分原因是能够访问这些系统的人太少了。 到目前为止，访问权限仅限于投资者和早期客户。 事实上，这就是 Etched 最初获得著名投资者名单的方式——在其办公室向他们展示私人演示。

Wachen 表示：“Anthropic 的 Andrej Karpathy、OpenAI 的 Noam Brown、Geoffrey Hinton 以及本轮融资的所有投资者——这些人都实际尝试过硬件，并且对此感到非常兴奋。”

尽管如此，在机架系统大规模生产和交付之前，这仍然是一条漫长而艰难的道路，还有更多的路要走。 这三人从哈佛退学并创立了 Etched，当时他们不知道如何筹集现金（更不用说他们需要的大量资金）或如何招聘。

“我们不知道这会有多困难，”瓦亨说。 “我认为我们仍然需要对真正实现规模化所需要的条件感到谦卑。”

瓦亨记得在告诉父母他要离开学校去创业后降落在湾区，没有安排办公室或公寓。 他睡在一个朋友没有家具的房子的地板上。

“我记得住在我朋友即将出售的房子里，用毛巾当毯子，”他笑着说。 创始人最终在一名早期员工的车库中设置了运行芯片设计工具所需的服务器，“每次需要重新启动时，他都会打电话给他的妻子，她会去按下重新启动按钮。”

如今，办公室里有 400 名员工忙碌，Etched 在那里运营着一个 2 兆瓦的数据中心。 该公司刚刚在米尔皮塔斯开设了一座占地 80,000 平方英尺、装机容量为 10 兆瓦的新工厂，位于圣何塞主要办事处的附近。

“我们今天在实验室里运行代币，与世界上一些最大的人工智能公司合作，”他说。

Wachen 现在也有了一条毯子、一张床垫，甚至还有一个枕头。 多个枕头，”他开玩笑说。

更重要的是，他和他的联合创始人从未让怀疑者阻止他们。 “已经走了很长一段路了。 这是一个非常非常不同的世界。 但我认为，当你真的认为某件事是可能的，并且你只要长期努力，你就能做到。”

注：本文已更新，包含对新 10MW 设施的引用。
